instructions: In `JustFile`, delete the `verify-fast` and `verify-full` recipes. Create a single new recipe called `verify` that runs all checks: `format lint check-types check-deps contracts test-unit`. Change the `default` recipe at the top of the file to point to `verify`. Then, in `.jitsurules`, update the execution environment rules to explicitly mandate `just verify` for execution, completely removing any references to `verify-fast`.
anti_patterns: Do not leave any dangling references to verify-fast or verify-full in the JustFile.
Strictly follow Ruff style guidelines when preparing python code.
There are no 'here's how you might do X' or similar boilerplate suggestions. Provide fully functioning code in code blocks.
If the edit requires only a single line (or two) change provide the context around it with the change clearly denoted so it is easy to drop in place.
completion_criteria: JustFile contains only a single `verify` pipeline.
.jitsurules explicitly instructs the agent to use `just verify`.

instructions: Audit `ContextCompiler` in `src/jitsu/core/compiler.py` and completely remove the 'Execution Environment' section injection that was added back in Epic 4.5. The compiler MUST NOT inject any hardcoded strings mentioning `uv run`, `just`, or `pytest`. It should strictly render the `instructions`, `anti_patterns`, `completion_criteria`, `verification_commands`, and the JIT Context. Update `tests/core/test_compiler.py` to remove any assertions expecting these hardcoded environment strings.
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode ANY repository-specific execution commands in the Python compiler.
Strictly follow Ruff style guidelines when preparing python code.
There are no 'here's how you might do X' or similar boilerplate suggestions. Provide fully functioning code in code blocks.
If the edit requires only a single line (or two) change provide the context around it with the change clearly denoted so it is easy to drop in place.
completion_criteria: The ContextCompiler is 100% agnostic to the underlying project's build tools.
All unit tests pass with the hardcoded strings removed.

instructions: Update `docs/jitsu_orchestrator_prompt.md`. Ensure the prompt instructs the LLM that when drafting epics for THIS repository, the standard verification command to inject into the `verification_commands` array is `just verify`. Remove any lingering examples of `verify-fast` or pure `pytest`.
anti_patterns: Do not alter the JSON schema definition in the orchestrator prompt.
completion_criteria: The orchestrator prompt correctly instructs the LLM to use `just verify` for repository-specific verification.
